items.summary: TO AMEND THE SALES AND USE TAX EXEMPTION FOR DATA CENTERS.
State Sales and Use Taxes; the data center equipment sales and use tax exemption; repeal
State Sales and Use Taxes; data center equipment sales and use tax exemption; advance the sunset date
Requiring data centers to be located on land that was zoned for industrial or manufacturing uses or was not zoned on July 1, 2025, to qualify for the sales tax exemption for qualified data centers.

Substitute for HB 2450 by Committee on Commerce, Labor and Economic Development - Providing a sales tax exemption for the construction or remodeling of a qualified data center in Kansas and the purchase of data center equipment, eligible data center costs, electricity and certain labor costs to qualified firms that commit to a minimum investment of at least $600,000,000 and meet new Kansas jobs and other requirements.

Providing a sales tax exemption for the construction or remodeling of a qualified data center in Kansas and the purchase of data center equipment, eligible data center costs, electricity and certain labor costs to qualified firms that commit to a minimum investment of at least $600,000,000 and meet new Kansas jobs and other requirements.
Sales and Use Tax Exemption – Qualified Data Center Personal Property – Eligibility

Change provisions relating to inheritance taxes, change certain fee and tax provisions, and eliminate a sales tax exemption relating to data centers
Eliminate a sales and use tax exemption relating to data centers

in research and development tax credit, further providing for credit for research and development expenses, for carryover, carryback, refund and assignment of credit and for report to General Assembly; An Act amending the act of March 4, 1971 (P.L.6, No.2), known as the Tax Reform Code of 1971,  in computer data center equipment incentive program, further providing for definitions and for review of application, providing for applicability and for sales and use tax exemption program; imposing duties on the Department of Revenue; and making editorial changes.
Removing a tax exemption for the replacement of equipment for data centers.

Providing a retail sales and use tax exemption for the construction and equipping of new data centers located in a county east of the Cascades that borders another state and has a population of at least 500,000.
Relating to: modifying the sales and use tax exemption for qualified data centers. (FE)
Relating to: modifying the sales and use tax exemption for qualified data centers. (FE)
Relating to: a sales and use tax exemption for data center equipment or software. (FE)
Relating to: a sales and use tax exemption for data center equipment or software. (FE)
Data center tax exemption-repeal.

Governor Mills signed LD 713, excluding data centers that begin operating after August 1, 2026 from Maine's Business Equipment Tax Exemption (BETE) and Dirigo Business Incentives programs. Signed the same day as the LD 307 veto.

Texas Comptroller projected the state's qualified data center sales-tax exemption will cost ~$3.2B in foregone revenue over the 2027-28 biennium, up from a 2022 estimate of $180M. Lawmakers say they will consider proposals in January 2027 session to limit or repeal the exemption.

Port Washington voters approved a referendum 66% in favor requiring voter approval for any TIF district over $10 million. Organized by Great Lakes Neighbors United in response to a $458 million TIF approved for Vantage's 672-acre 'Lighthouse' campus partnered with OpenAI and Oracle.

Meta officially confirmed it is the developer of 'Project Anthem,' a $1B 340-acre data center in east Tulsa. Meta will receive an annual 85% property tax exemption; the remaining 15% projected to raise $36M over 25-year incentive district. 100 operational + 1,000 peak-construction jobs.

H 5286 would prohibit governmental entities from granting final approval for permits, applications, incentives, rezoning, special exceptions, building permits, site plans, fee-in-lieu of taxes, or any other form of incentive for new data centers until January 1, 2028.

TigerDC withdrew 'Project Spero,' a proposed $3 billion AI data center at Tyger River Industrial Park, after Spartanburg County Council members signaled they would vote against the property tax incentive package. Opposition included a 4,000-signature petition and ~3,000-member 'No Data Centers in Spartanburg' Facebook group.

The Energy and Technology Committee held a public hearing on SB 245, which would revoke the data center tax incentives signed into law in 2021 (PA 21-1). Reflects growing legislative skepticism after Millstone hyperscale proposal collapsed.

AWS expanded its Madison County 'Project Atlas' commitment to $11B across two industrial parks covering 1,700 acres and 1,000 jobs, part of $25B total Mississippi investment also including Warren and Hinds counties. Entergy Mississippi launched Superpower Mississippi Initiative with $300M in grid investments.

Alabama Senate unanimously passed HB 399 (Rep. Leigh Hulsey), sending it to Gov. Kay Ivey, capping data center tax abatements at 20 years (down from 30) effective Jan. 1, 2027. Operators can earn back 10 years to reach 30 by entering binding 'qualified local investment' agreements covering roads, broadband, water/wastewater, or education.

Arkansas enacted Act 548 (HB 1444), expanding state sales/use tax exemption for data centers by lowering qualifying investment threshold from $500M to $100M and adding 'Qualified Large Data Centers' tier (multi-site fiber-linked) for projects exceeding $2B. Effective Oct. 1, 2025.

Ravenna City Council approved 12-month moratorium on new data center development after nearly 100 residents packed April 10 council meeting voicing concerns about water, ratepayer impacts, and tax burden shifting. Kent and Shalersville passed similar moratoriums.

House Bill 710, introduced in 2025-2026 Ohio General Assembly, would prohibit public support for and limit construction of new data centers. Reflects growing legislative response to constituent frustration over data center build-out.

House Bill 1210 passed and heads to Gov. Mike Braun, requiring data center companies to pay 1% of savings from total electricity bill to qualify for sales tax breaks. HB 1333 (would have stripped local zoning authority) died in committee after public backlash.

Amazon scrapped plans for a large data center project in Becker. Microsoft, which had signed an NDA with Becker, announced March 18 it would end its use of NDAs with local governments altogether amid statewide pushback.
Independence voters ousted city council members Bridget McCandless and Jared Fears after they voted to grant more than $6B in tax breaks to a Nebius AI data center in eastern Independence.

Kansas enacted SB 98, a 20-year sales and use tax exemption for qualified data centers investing at least $250M, covering construction, equipment, labor and backup generators. Took effect July 1, 2025.

Washington's Legislature passed SB 6231, eliminating sales-tax break on equipment and labor used to refurbish existing data centers. Department of Revenue estimates change will generate $63.1M in current biennium and $143.9M in 2027-29.
Oregon lawmakers amended HB 4084 (Gov. Kotek's enterprise-zone bill) to impose one-year moratorium barring data centers from receiving new or expanded Standard Enterprise Zone tax subsidies.

Gov. Katie Hobbs included repeal of Arizona's data center sales-tax incentive program in FY26 executive budget; Rep. Neal Carter introduced bill to halt new applications at year-end. 2013 program cost state $38.5M in FY2025.
